content: Just as human language learning of the word 'reflexion' encodes layered meanings (reflection of light, reflection on thought) that learners gradually refine through repeated contextual exposure, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be structured to iteratively refine the multi-layered semantic and operational meaning of their own problem-solving outputs, rather than only correcting factual errors. This framing turns LLM self-reflection into a developmental learning process analogous to how humans acquire nuanced, multi-use vocabulary.
